title: Segment Atlas: an Unsupervised Customer Segmentation Assigner
strategies: coordinate-threshold split: sort customers by their first feature and cut into k equal ranked bins -- structure-blind, reproduces the evaluator's weak reference (~0.1)
k-means (Lloyd + k-means++): centroid clustering on the raw 2-D features; recovers convex blob spend-tiers well but shreds the non-convex moon arcs and concentric ring shells

normalised spectral clustering: build a kNN Gaussian-affinity graph, embed via the top-k eigenvectors of the symmetric-normalised affinity, then k-means the row-normalised embedding -- follows connectivity/manifold structure so it recovers blobs, moons AND rings on aggregate, with headroom left by boundary noise
density / connectivity methods (single-linkage, DBSCAN, mutual-kNN components) that chain along manifolds -- strong on clean shapes but fragile to noise bridges, a viable alternative family
